architecture_summary: HAT is a Hybrid Attention Transformer for single image super-resolution that combines channel attention with window-based self-attention, and adds an overlapping cross-attention module to strengthen cross-window interaction. It also uses a same-task pre-training strategy on ImageNet before fine-tuning on SR datasets.

novel_contributions: We propose a novel Hybrid Attention Transformer (HAT) that combines self-attention, channel attention and a new overlapping cross-attention to activate more pixels for better reconstruction.
We propose an effective same-task pre-training strategy to further exploit the potential of SR Transformer and show the importance of large-scale data pre-training for the task.
By further scaling up HAT to build a big model, we greatly extend the performance upper bound of the SR task.

key_claims.claim: Transformer-based SR networks can only utilize a limited spatial range of input information according to attribution analysis, motivating HAT.
HAT combines channel attention and window-based self-attention, and adds overlapping cross-attention to better aggregate cross-window information.
The paper introduces a same-task pre-training strategy that pre-trains on a large-scale dataset for the same SR task before fine-tuning.
HAT significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods by more than 1dB.
Using a larger window size of 16 × 16 improves performance and increases the range of used pixels.
OCAB and CAB each improve Urban100 ×4 SR, and together yield the best DI and SSIM in the ablation study.
HAT outperforms SwinIR and EDT on all benchmark datasets in Table 6, with larger gains on Urban100 and Manga109.
Same-task pre-training performs better than multi-related-task pre-training for HAT.
